title: Result - United-kingdom
content:
Welcome to the United Kingdom, a union of England, Scotland, Wales and Northern Ireland, where over 160 nationalities speak more than 300 languages.
The UK boasts stunning landscapes, from misty moors and sandy coves to rugged coastlines and ancient woodlands. Explore London’s landmarks, centuries of history in castles and museums, or hike in one of 15 national parks. Enjoy a traditional afternoon tea, camp in the hills, or visit iconic sites like Big Ben, Tower Bridge, Stonehenge, the Roman Baths and the historic universities of Oxford and Cambridge.

## Unique food tradition
Scotch egg: a soft-boiled egg wrapped in sausage, then coated in breadcrumbs and baked or fried.
80% of the UK's economy is dominated by services industries, which include retail, hospitality and finance, as well as public services like health and education.

### Local legend or myth
The Loch Ness Monster: Scotland’s most famous mythical creature, the Loch Ness Monster, or “Nessie,” is said to live in Loch Ness, though its existence remains unproven.

### Language of the Land
English is spoken across the UK, but it is not the only native official language. You may also hear Welsh in Wales and Gaelic or Scots in Scotland.
million cups of tea are consumed daily in the UK on average.
